stem: A decision-maker must choose among 4 project methodology options. The probability of favorable team experience and project size is uncertain, estimated between 32% and 47%. The payoffs (in $K) for each option are: Agile: $210K in good team experience and project size, $21K in bad team experience and project size. Waterfall: $209K in good team experience and project size, $93K in bad team experience and project size. Hybrid: $210K in good team experience and project size, $35K in bad team experience and project size. Kanban: $254K in good team experience and project size, $60K in bad team experience and project size. Which option maximizes expected value? Does the optimal choice change across the range of probability estimates? What is the expected value of the best option?

ground_truth.answer: At P(good)=0.4: best option is Waterfall with EV=$139.4K. The optimal choice switches: Waterfall at P(good)=0.32, Kanban at P(good)=0.47. EV range of best option: [130.1, 151.2].

solution_steps: Compute EV for each option: EV = P(good)×payoff_good + P(bad)×payoff_bad.
The optimal choice switches: Waterfall at P(good)=0.32, Kanban at P(good)=0.47.
common_failure_modes: Evaluating options only at the midpoint probability without sensitivity analysis
Not identifying the breakeven probability where the optimal choice switches
Ignoring downside risk and focusing only on expected value
